headline: Volvo teases a new affordable EV to replace discontinued EX30
summary: Volvo is planning an affordable EV to succeed the discontinued EX30. The EX30 faced issues like high tariffs and a battery recall. This new model reaffirms Volvo's commitment to budget-friendly electric vehicles.

headline: PlayStation exclusives aren't coming to PC anymore
summary: Sony is reportedly halting the release of major single-player PlayStation games on PC. Hermen Hulst, head of PlayStation studios, informed employees of this strategy change. This reverses previous plans to expand PC releases.

headline: Musk v. Altman proved that AI is led by the wrong people
summary: The Musk v. Altman 'tech trial' was a power struggle over AI's future. Elon Musk challenged Sam Altman's leadership at OpenAI. The jury reached a verdict after a short deliberation.

headline: Walmart launches new budget-friendly Android tablets starting at $97
summary: Walmart's Onn brand released a new line of budget-friendly Android tablets. Six new models launched, most priced under $200. These tablets ship with Android 16, offering an affordable alternative to premium devices.

headline: Beat the Summer Heat With This $9 Portable Turbo Fan During Amazon's Memorial Day Sale
summary: A $9 portable turbo fan is available during Amazon's Memorial Day Sale. It offers a budget-friendly way to stay cool this summer. The deal helps combat the heat effectively.

headline: PlayStation Reportedly Tells Developers That It Is Returning to Console Exclusives for Single-Player Games
summary: PlayStation is reportedly reverting to a console-exclusive strategy for single-player titles. Developers were informed of this shift, indicating a change in their PC release plans. Sony seems to be stepping back from its recent strategy of bringing exclusives to PC.

headline: PlayStation Console Owners Launch Class-Action Lawsuit Against Sony Over Tariff Refunds
summary: PlayStation users have initiated a class-action lawsuit against Sony. The lawsuit seeks refunds due to PS5 price increases caused by tariffs. Fans are challenging Sony's previous pricing adjustments.

headline: Stranger Things Creators Did a Half Day of Reshoots Just to 'Milk' Steve's Fake-Out Death
summary: Stranger Things creators, the Duffer Brothers, admitted to extra reshoots for Steve's fake-out death. They extended the scene to intentionally 'f**k with the audience'. This decision aimed to heighten viewer suspense and emotional impact.

headline: Married at First Sight UK brides tell BBC they were raped by on-screen husbands
summary: Brides from Married at First Sight UK reported rape allegations against their on-screen husbands to the BBC. Channel 4 was aware of at least one claim before the show aired. Despite the claims, the involved woman still featured in the broadcast.

headline: New High Street crime unit to target gangs fronting shops after BBC investigation
summary: A new High Street crime unit will target gangs operating through shop fronts. This unit follows a BBC investigation exposing various criminal activities. The investigation revealed drug gangs, money laundering, and immigration crimes linked to these businesses.

headline: Trump says he called off new Iran attack at request of Gulf states
summary: Former President Trump stated he canceled a planned US attack on Iran. He claimed the decision was made at the request of Gulf states. Trump noted that 'serious negotiations are now taking place'.

headline: Faisal Islam: Burnham seeks to calm markets by committing to fiscal rules
summary: Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham is working to reassure markets. He is committing to fiscal rules to manage economic perceptions. This move aims to stabilize markets alongside local election efforts.
